documentation.description:
This package provides encoding-aware output to and input from text files and text streams. Text streams are byte
streams that can be read or written sequentially, and where the bytes constitute text encoded in some character
encoding. They have no defined line length; line breaks are represented as characters in the stream. There is no
concept of pages in this package.

Each open stream has an associated character encoding. By default, streams are assumed to be encoded in the
encoding that OS.OS_Encoding reports. Another encoding can be specified when the stream is opened or later. How
line breaks are represented when writing and the set of line breaks that shall be recognized when reading can
also be configured.

In this package it is assumed that Ada.Streams.Stream_Element equals one byte, that is, eight bits.

simple_types.name: Read_Stop_Cause
simple_types.code:
type Read_Stop_Cause is
   (All_Done,
    End_Of_File,
    Incomplete,
    Invalid);

documentation.description:
Reason why Get or Get_Line returns.
  All_Done    : The requested amount of data was read.
  End_Of_File : The end of the file was reached before the requested
                amount of data was read.
  Incomplete  : The file ended in an incomplete code sequence.
  Invalid     : The file's content wasn't a valid encoded text for the
                file's encoding.

tagged_types.name: EAfile
tagged_types.code: type EAfile is new EAstream with private;

documentation.description: An EAfile reads from or writes to a named external file.

tagged_types.name: EAstream
tagged_types.code: type EAstream is abstract tagged limited private;

tagged_types.name: Stream_EAstream
tagged_types.code: type Stream_EAstream is new EAstream with private;

documentation.description:
A Stream_EAstream reads and writes through an object in Root_Stream_Type'Class, so that you can wrap an EAstream
around anything that you can get a Stream_Access value for.

access_types.name: EAstream_Pointer
access_types.code: type EAstream_Pointer is access all EAstream'Class;

documentation.description: The file's content wasn't a valid encoded text for the file's encoding.
Set_Recognized_Line_Breaks was called with a string that wasn't one or two characters long.

subprograms.name: Append_File
subprograms.code: function Append_File return File_Mode

subprograms.name: Close
subprograms.code: procedure Close (File : in out EAfile)

documentation.description:
Severs the association between the given EAfile and its associated external file. The EAfile is left closed. If
the file being closed has mode Out_File or Append_File, then the last character written since the most recent
open or reset is the last character that can be read from the file. If nothing has been written and the mode
is Out_File, then the closed file is empty. If nothing has been written and the mode is Append_File, then the
closed file is unchanged.

Status_Error is propagated if the EAfile is not open.

subprograms.name: Connect

documentation.description:
Connects an EAstream object to an open byte stream and sets the character encoding to be used when reading from
or writing to the stream.

subprograms.name: Create

documentation.description:
Creates a new external file, with the given name and form, and associates this external file with the given
EAfile. The EAfile is left open. The character encoding of the EAfile is set to the given encoding and its
current mode is set to Out_File.

A null string for Name specifies an external file that is not accessible after the completion of the main
program (a temporary file). The valid values of Form are the same as for Ada.Streams.Stream_IO.Create; a
null string specifies the default options for the external file.

Status_Error is propagated if the EAfile is already open. Name_Error is propagated if the string given as Name
does not allow the identification of an external file. Use_Error is propagated if the external environment does
not support creation of an external file with the given name (in the absence of Name_Error) and form.

subprograms.name: Delete
subprograms.code: procedure Delete (File : in out EAfile)

documentation.description:
Deletes the external file associated with the given EAfile. The EAfile is closed, and the external file ceases
to exist.

Status_Error is propagated if the EAfile is not open. Use_Error is propagated if deletion of the external file
is not supported by the external environment.

subprograms.name: Encoding
subprograms.code: function Encoding (File : in EAstream) return Character_Encoding

documentation.description:
Returns the character encoding that the byte stream is currently interpreted as when reading from File, or that
the text is currently encoded in when writing to File.

subprograms.name: End_Of_File
subprograms.code: function End_Of_File (File : in EAstream) return Boolean

documentation.description:
Returns True if the end of the file has been found, otherwise returns False.

End_Of_File can't look ahead. It only returns True if Get or Get_Line has already encountered the end of the
file. It is quite possible that the last character has already been read but End_Of_File returns False, because
only the next read attempt will fail and report the end of the file.

subprograms.name: Flush
subprograms.code: procedure Flush (File : in EAfile)

documentation.description:
Synchronizes the external file with the internal file (by flushing any internal buffers) without closing the
file or changing the position. Mode_Error is propagated if the mode of the EAfile is In_File.

subprograms.name: Form
subprograms.code: function Form (File : in EAfile) return String

documentation.description:
Returns the form string for the external file currently associated with the given EAfile. Status_Error is
propagated if the EAfile is not open.

subprograms.name: Get
subprograms.code: procedure Get (Item : out EAstring; Length : in Natural)

documentation.description: Like above, but reads from Standard_Input.

subprograms.name: Get

documentation.description:
Like above, but raises exceptions when reading fails: End_Error is raised if an attempt is made to read a file
terminator. Incomplete_Byte_Sequence is raised if File ends in an incomplete code sequence.

Invalid_Byte_Sequence is raised if File's content isn't a valid encoded text for File's encoding.

subprograms.name: Get

documentation.description:
Reads the number of characters specified by Length from File and stores them in Item. If line breaks are
encountered they will be included in Item just like other characters.

Mode_Error is propagated if File is an EAfile whose mode is not In_File. Otherwise, success or failure is
reported in Exit_Cause. In case of failure, as much as could be read is returned in Item.

subprograms.name: Get_Line
subprograms.code: procedure Get_Line (Item : out EAstring)

documentation.description: Like above, but reads from Standard_Input.

subprograms.name: Get_Line
subprograms.code: procedure Get_Line (File : in out EAstream; Item : out EAstring)

documentation.description:
Like above, but raises exceptions when reading fails: End_Error is raised if the file ends before anything has
been read. Incomplete_Byte_Sequence is raised if File ends in an incomplete code sequence.

Invalid_Byte_Sequence is raised if File's content isn't a valid encoded text for File's encoding.

subprograms.name: Get_Line

documentation.description:
Reads characters from File and stores them in Item until one of the recognized line breaks is encountered. In
the next call to Get or Get_Line, the first character read will be the first one after the line break.

Mode_Error is propagated if File is an EAfile whose mode is not In_File. Otherwise, success or failure is
reported in Exit_Cause. In case of failure, as much as could be read is returned in Item.

If the file ends before anything has been read, Exit_Cause is set to End_Of_File. If characters have been read,
Exit_Cause is set to All_Done and End_Of_File is not reported until the next read attempt. That is, it is not
required that a file ends with a line break.

subprograms.name: In_File
subprograms.code: function In_File return File_Mode

subprograms.name: Is_Open
subprograms.code: function Is_Open (File : in EAstream) return Boolean

documentation.description: Returns True if the EAstream is open (that is, if it is associated with a byte stream), otherwise returns False.

subprograms.name: Line_Break
subprograms.code: function Line_Break (File : in EAstream) return EAstring

documentation.description: Returns the string that is currently used for line breaks when Put_Line or New_Line is called on File.

subprograms.name: Mode
subprograms.code: function Mode (File : in EAfile) return File_Mode

documentation.description: Returns the current mode of the given EAfile. Status_Error is propagated if the EAfile is not open.

subprograms.name: Name
subprograms.code: function Name (File : in EAfile) return EAstring

documentation.description:
Returns a string which uniquely identifies the external file currently associated with the given EAfile (and may
thus be used in an Open operation).

Status_Error is propagated if the EAfile is not open. Use_Error is propagated if the associated external file is
a temporary file that cannot be opened by any name.

subprograms.name: New_Line
subprograms.code: procedure New_Line (File : in EAstream; Spacing : in Positive := 1)

documentation.description:
Writes the number of line breaks specified by Spacing to File. Mode_Error is propagated if File is an EAfile
whose mode is not Out_File or Append_File.

subprograms.name: New_Line
subprograms.code: procedure New_Line (Spacing : in Positive := 1)

documentation.description: Like above, but writes to Standard_Output.

subprograms.name: Open

documentation.description:
Associates the given EAfile with an existing external file having the given name and form, and sets the current
mode and character encoding of the EAfile to the given mode and encoding. The EAfile is left open.

The valid values of Form are the same as for Ada.Streams.Stream_IO.Open.

Status_Error is propagated if the EAfile is already open. Name_Error is propagated if the string given as
Name does not allow the identification of an external file; in particular, this exception is propagated if
no external file with the given name exists. Use_Error is propagated if, for the specified mode, the external
environment does not support opening for an external file with the given name (in the absence of Name_Error)
and form.

subprograms.name: Out_File
subprograms.code: function Out_File return File_Mode

subprograms.name: Put
subprograms.code: procedure Put (Item : in EAstring)

documentation.description: Like above, but writes to Standard_Output.

subprograms.name: Put
subprograms.code: procedure Put (File : in EAstream; Item : in EAstring)

documentation.description:
Writes the string in Item to File. Mode_Error is propagated if File is an EAfile whose mode is not Out_File or
Append_File.

subprograms.name: Put_Line
subprograms.code: procedure Put_Line (Item : in EAstring)

documentation.description: Like above, but writes to Standard_Output.

subprograms.name: Put_Line
subprograms.code: procedure Put_Line (File : in EAstream; Item : in EAstring)

documentation.description:
Writes the string in Item to File, followed by a line break. Mode_Error is propagated if File is an EAfile whose
mode is not Out_File or Append_File.

subprograms.name: Recognized_Line_Breaks
subprograms.code: function Recognized_Line_Breaks (File : in EAstream) return EAstring_Array

documentation.description:
Returns the set of strings that are currently recognized as line breaks by Get_Line and Skip_Line when reading
from File.

subprograms.name: Reset
subprograms.code: procedure Reset (File : in out EAfile)

documentation.description: Like above, but does not change the character encoding or the mode.

subprograms.name: Reset
subprograms.code: procedure Reset (File : in out EAfile; Mode : in File_Mode)

documentation.description: Like above, but does not change the character encoding.

subprograms.name: Reset

documentation.description:
Resets the given EAfile so that reading can be restarted from the beginning of the file (for the mode In_File),
and so that writing can be restarted at the beginning of the file (for the mode Out_File) or after the last
character of the file (for the mode Append_File). The current mode and character encoding of the EAfile are set
to the given mode and encoding. If the EAfile has mode Out_File or Append_File when Reset is called, the last
character written since the most recent open or reset is the last character that can be read from the file. If
nothing has been written and the mode is Out_File, then the reset file is empty. If nothing has been written and
the mode is Append_File, then the reset file is unchanged.

Status_Error is propagated if the EAfile is not open. Use_Error is propagated if the external environment does
not support resetting for the external file and, also, if the external environment does not support resetting
to the specified mode for the external file.

subprograms.name: Set_Encoding
subprograms.code: procedure Set_Encoding (File : in out EAstream; Encoding : in Character_Encoding)

documentation.description:
Sets the character encoding that the byte stream should be interpreted as when reading from File, or that the
text should be encoded in when writing to File. The character encoding can be changed in the middle of the file
if so desired.

subprograms.name: Set_Line_Break
subprograms.code: procedure Set_Line_Break (File : in out EAstream; Line_Break : in EAstring)

documentation.description: Sets the string to be written for line breaks when Put_Line or New_Line is called on File.

subprograms.name: Set_Recognized_Line_Breaks
subprograms.code: procedure Set_Recognized_Line_Breaks (File : in out EAstream; Line_Breaks : in EAstring_Array)

documentation.description: Sets the set of strings to be recognized as line breaks by Get_Line and Skip_Line when reading from File.

subprograms.name: Skip_Line
subprograms.code: procedure Skip_Line (File : in out EAstream; Spacing : in Positive := 1)

documentation.description:
Reads from File and discards all characters until the number of line breaks specified by Spacing has been read.

Mode_Error is propagated if File is an EAfile whose mode is not In_File. End_Error is propagated if an attempt
is made to read a file terminator. Incomplete_Byte_Sequence is propagated if File ends in an incomplete code
sequence.

Invalid_Byte_Sequence is propagated if File's content isn't a valid encoded text for File's encoding.

subprograms.name: Skip_Line
subprograms.code: procedure Skip_Line (Spacing : in Positive := 1)

documentation.description: Like above, but reads from Standard_Input.

subprograms.name: Standard_Error
subprograms.code: function Standard_Error return EAstream_Pointer

subprograms.name: Standard_Input
subprograms.code: function Standard_Input return EAstream_Pointer

subprograms.name: Standard_Output
subprograms.code: function Standard_Output return EAstream_Pointer

subprograms.name: Stream
subprograms.code: function Stream (File : in EAstream) return Stream_IO.Stream_Access

documentation.description:
Returns a pointer to the underlying raw byte stream that File is associated with. Status_Error is propagated if
File is not open.
